wety: From Classical Persian مهران (mihrān). Cognate with Punjabi مہران (mehrān) and Sindhi مَهَراڻُ (maharāṇu), Persian borrowings. An ancient Persianate name for the Indus, it was already in use during the earliest medieval era. The accomplished Arab grammarian Ibn Duraid in the 9ᵗʰ century used the name to describe the "great river" in what is today Pakistan.

more: n. f. pl. of محسوسه Things felt or perceived; things perceptible to the senses.

wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مهتاب (mah-tāb).

def: n. f. 1. A kind of marshmelon. 2. A shaddock ( chakotrā ). 3. Cloth on which are pasted devices of the heavenly bodies in gold or silver. 4. A blue light; a firework.

ety: mahtāb, q.v.+ī = S. इन्
def: adj. Of or belonging to the moon; — of moonshine; bright as the moon; like the moon; — s.f. A kind of fireworks (the light of which resembles that of the moon), a blue-light; — a fruit of the lime kind, pumpelmoose, shaddock; — brocade; — an open high terrace.
1. s. f. A kind of firework, blue-light. 2. A sort of muskmelon. 3. (dakh.) Cloth on which are pasted devices of the heavenly bodies in gold or silver. ( Qanoon-e-islam ).
1. adj. Belonging to the moon, like the moon, of moonshine. 2. s. f. A kind of fireworks. 3. An open high terrace. 4. (dakh.) Brocade ( Harris ).

def: s.f. Mother (i.q. mā, or māʼī).
n. f. S. मातृ A mother. Mā kī sauk na bāp se yārī, Kis nāte kī tauṅh mahtārī? Rus. Prov. Nor co-wife nor my father's mistress yet, how then my mother who did me beget?

s.m. = mahto, q.v. s.m. Headman of a village; the person employed by a landholder to collect the rent from a village, al and-bailif; a factor, or agent; a head (village) peon, a village constable; a scribe, a clerk (also written mahtauṅ).

s. m. A person employed by the landholder to collect the rent from a village, a land-bailiff.
s.m. A kind of scolopendra (syn. kan-salāʼī).

n. m. मधूक Pr. mahuā . The broad-leaved Bassia latifolia . The nuts yield a substance with which butter is adulterated, and from the flowers of which a spirituous liquor is obtained by distillation. E. Ādmī meṅ nauā, panchhī meṅ kauā, Lakṛī meṅ jhauā, phūloṅ meṅ mauā, E chāroṅ be-kamauā Bhoj. Prov. A barber among men, a crow 'mong birds, the fir 'mong trees, the mahuā among flowers, these four are worst among the base. mahūe kā tel , The oil of the mahuā . mahūe kī sharāb , Spirits made from mahūā .
more: s. m. Name of a tree (Bassia latifolia) bearing flowers which are sweet, and from which a spirituous liquor is distilled. The nuts afford an oil used instead of butter.

ety: Prk. महुओ, महुअं, or महूओ, महूअं; S. मधूकः, मधूकं

subs: s.m. Oil extracted from the seed of the mahuʼā
Spirituous liquor distilled from the flower of the mahuʼā.

subs: s.m. Oil extracted from the seed of the mahuʼā
Spirituous liquor distilled from the flower of the mahuʼā.

def: E. n. m. Poison ( bis ). Mān kā māhur, aur apmān kā laḍḍū . Prov. Rather poison with respect than dainties with contempt.

n. m. S. महूर्त 1. A division of time equal to 12 chhins or 48 minutes. 2. An auspicious moment. mahūrat bichārnā, yā dekhnā , v. a. To ascertain the auspicious moment for any work. Dekh mahūrat, ho pāṅḍā! Kā din jiu taje ḍhāṅḍā? Rus. O priest! look for the auspicious day when this old thing shall pass away. mahūrat k. , v. a. To begin a work in an auspicious
more: s. m. A division of time, equal to 12 ksh̤aṇas or 48 minutes.

subs: s.m. Oil extracted from the seed of the mahuʼā
Spirituous liquor distilled from the flower of the mahuʼā.

def: s.m. Erasing, erasure; cancelling; effacing, obliterating, obliteration; abolition; extinction; — adj. Erased, effaced, obliterated; forgotten; abolished; annihilated; — overpowered (by), struck or astonished, thunder-struck; fascinated, charmed, captivated; mad (from love), distracted (with terror or grief); — engrossed, absorbed, wrapt (in)
more: adj. 1. Effaced; erased; obliterated. 2. Fascinated; charmed; captivated. 3. Engrossed; absorbed, wrapt in. mahv h. , v. n. 1. To be effaced, etc. ( bhūlnā , 1). 2. To be fascinated, charmed. 3. To be absorbed, wrapped.

(Heb. Chald. ) adj. Effaced, forgotten, erased, obliterated, abolished. s. m. Effacing, obliteration, erasure, abolition, oblivion, extinction. محو کرنا maḥv karnā, To efface, deface, erase, obliterate.
subs: Struck or astonished at the sight (of, -kā), overpowered by the appearance (of)

s.m. A fish's tooth (of which the hilts of swords, &c. are made)
s.m. A fisherman; — an otter, Lutra lutreola

s.m. Certain honours of which the privilege is especially conferred on princes and great nobles (they are denoted by the figure of a fish with other insignia (two balls), carried as ensigns upon elephants)

def: s. m. Buttermilk.

ety: inf. n. of محض 'to possess pure milk, or milk unmixed with water,' &c.
def: adj. & adv. Pure, unmixed, unalloyed; only, mere, simple, sheer, absolute, entire; — purely; merely; solely, entirely, utterly, absolutely, quite
more: adj. 1. Pure; unmixed. 2. Absolute; entire; mere. mahz , adv. Absolutely; utterly; downright (بالکل & صرف). mahz dastkhat . A blank endorsement. mahz dastkhat k. , v. a. To endorse in blank; to give carte blanche. mahz gair-mumkin . Absolutely impossible. mahz fiqreh-bāzī . Sheer device or trickery. mahz qaid , G. G. Simple imprisonment.
1. adj. Pure, unmixed. 2. adv. Merely, entirely, wholly, purely, solely.
subs: (-par), To put nothing but the signature (on); to endorse in blank; to give carte blanche
A mere playing with words; sheer device or trickery
s.f. A mere nothing, a mere trifle.

ety: n. of place fr. حضر 'to be or become present,' &c.
def: s.f. A place where people are present or assembled; royal presence; appearance; — people present or assembled; an assembly; — a document or petition attested by a number of witnesses laid before a judge (with the view of promoting a suit); a public attestation
more: n. f. حضر was present. A public attestation. Dikhāë lākh voh shāh o vazīr kā mahzar Sanad na rakkhegā koī faqīr kā mahzar . Vazir or king's sign manual though a lakh times shown Zafar. Credentials of a beggar poor no one will own.

(noun of place from حضر) s. f. Royal presence, appearance, a general application or petition, a representation, statement of a case or suit laid before a judge, &c. محضر نامه maḥẓar-nāma, s. m. A muster-roll, list of those who are present.
subs: s.m. 'Place of assembly'; a court a police-station
(-kī), To present or put in a petition, or a public attestation (of)
s.m. A muster-roll, a list of the persons present at an assembly; — a public attestation, &c. ( = maḥẓar).

part. pass. (of حذف) Taken away (as, a syllable from a word).

def: s.m. pl. (of maḥz̤ūr), Interdicted or unlawful things.

more: adj. Pleased (خوش 1). mahzūz h. , v. n. To be pleased.
(part. pass. of حظّ) adj. Glad, cheerful, contented, delighted, pleased, happy.

s. f. 1. A harrow. 2. A ladder.

ety: corr. fr. the English
def: s.f. May (the month).

ety: Prk. माइआ; S. मातृका
def: s.f. Mother, &c. ( = mā, q.v.); — a woman's breast. s.m. (in Prosody) A molossus or metrical foot of three long syllables; — a magical or mystical formula; a name of Brahmā, of Vishṇu, and of Śiva.
more: n. f. 1. A woman; mother. 1. Māiyāṅ to bahuterī milī haiṅ, bābū koī nahīṅ milā thā . Prov. You have encountered women, strong ones you have yet to meet. (Said to a boaster). 2. Māī bāp ke lātan hī māreṅ, mehrī dekh juṛāëṅ, Chāroṅ dhām jo phirī āve tabahūṅ pāp na jāë . Who kicks his parents to give pleasure to his wife, His sin will cleave to him though a pilgrim all his life. 3. Jaisī māīvaisijāī . Prov. The daughter as the mother. 2. A mother goddess. 3. A respectful address to a female. māī bāp , 1. Father and mother. 2. Master; lord. Bachnā, māī bāp! (Sweepers). māī kā lāl, yā beṭā , n. m. 1. Mother's darling son. 2. A beautiful and brave young man of noble bearing. 3. A godly person. Ādhī roṭī, us par khīr, māī kā beṭā huā amīr . Mendicants. Half a cake with rice-milk on it thrown, the mother's darling son to greatness grown. māī kā thān , n. m. A temple to a goddess.

ety: S. मदिका or मत्य+इका; Prk. मइआ?
def: s.f. A harrow; — a ladder.
wety: Borrowed from English May, ultimately from Latin Maius.

ety: dialec.
def: pron. (old H.) = maiṅ, q.v. s.f. Maternal uncle's wife, aunt ( = māmī); — a respectful term of address to elderly women (prob. for māʼī).
more: s. f. Wine, spirituous liquor. مي آشام mai-āshām, A wine-drinker. مي پرست mai-parast, A lover of wine, a drunkard. مي پرستي mai-parast-ī, s. f. Love of wine. ميخانه mai-ḵẖāna or مي کده mai-kada, A tavern. مي خور mai-ḵẖor or مي خوار mai-ḵẖẉār, A sot, a wine-bibber. مي خوري mai-ḵẖor-ī or مي خواري mai-ḵẖẉār-ī or مي خوارگي mai-ḵẖẉāra-gī, s. f. Wine-drinking, drunkenness. مي کش mai-kash, A wine-bibber, a drunkard. مي کشي mai-kash-ī, s. f. Wine-drinking, carousal. ميگسار mai-gusār, Wine-drinker. مي گون mai-gūn, Auburne, fair, wine-coloured, light red. مي ناب mai-nāb, Pure wine. مي نوش mai-nosh, Wine-drinker. مي نوشي mai-nosh-ī, s. f. Wine-drinking.
(in compos.) Composed of, chiefly consisting or made of.

def: s.f. The flowing out or forth (of anything poured out); the beginning (of a thing); inchoate state; prime; — beginning of day, morning; beginning, or prime, or bloom, of youth.
more: s. The beginning of day or of youth. (Plur. ميعات).

def: s.f. pl. of maiʻat, q.v.

def: s.f. Stepmother.
s.m. = ميده maida, q.v. s.m. A table (esp. one covered with victuals).

more: n. m. Flour; meal. maidā sā , adj. Soft as cornflour; fine. maidā k. , v. a. To grind very fine. maidā-lakṛī , n. f. A root resembling ginger. maidā o shahāb , 1. Red and white. 2. Very beautiful. maide kī loī , Poet. A smooth soft belly.
s. m. Flower (of grain), especially of the purest sort or twice sifted.
subs: adj. Like fine flour; fine, or soft, as flour
adj. (f. -kī), Of fine flour; like fine flour; soft as fine flour
To convert into fine flour; to grind very fine
White (as flour) and red (as the juice of the kājīra); — very beautiful.

def: s.m. = ميڙا maiṛā, q.v. s.m. (dialec.) A kind of platform or scaffold erected in a field (where a person is stationed to watch the crops); — a ramp; — a kind of harrow
subs: (with loc.) To pass the harrow over a field (in order to break the clods)
To take a turn, to turn over on the side (in sleeping).

ety: for A. مائدة māʼidat, fem.
def: s.m. A table (esp. one covered with victuals).

ety: v.n. fr. ماد (for ميد
def: s.m. An open field (without buildings); an extensive plain; a plain, field, lawn, area; a race-ground; any place for exercise or walking; a parade; a field of battle; — war, battle, conflict; — a scene (in a play, or poem, &c.); — body, ground, middle part (of a garment, &c.)
more: n. m. See کهيت 1, 2, 4. 1. A plain. 2. A scene in a play, poem, etc. Qisse kā maidān . 3. A battle. Do maidān hue. maidān jānā , Rus. v. n. To go to the rear. maidān-i-jaṅg,yā kārzār , S. रणक्षेत्र Field of battle. maidān chhoṛnā , v. n. To take flight; to fly. maidān denā , v. a. To make room. maidān k . 1. To raze to the ground. 2. To fight. maidān mārnā , v. a. To win a battle. maidān meṅ , adv. In the open air or field. maidān meṅ ānā , v. n. To come out (to fight). qalam kā maidān , n. m. The shaft of a pen.
s. m. 1. A plain, open field, area. 2. A field of battle, parade. ميدان کشي maidān-kash-ī, Drawing out for battle.
subs: v.n. To go to the rear
or maidān-ě-kār-zār, s.m. Field of battle (syn. raṇ-kshetr)
To fly from the field of battle; to take flight
(-ko), To give room (to), give a wide berth (to); to make room (for)
v.t. To convert into a level plain; to raze to the ground; — to wage war, to fight
(or qalam-kā maidān), s.m. The shaft, or the nib, of a pen
adv. In the plain; in the open
To come into the plain, or field; to come out (to fight).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مَیْدَان (maydān), borrowed from Arabic مَيْدَان (maydān).

ety: maida, q.v.+ānī = Zend āna or ana+i = S. अन+इ
def: s.f. Flour made into dough for the preparation of sweetmeats.

more: s. m. 1. A person who precedes a general or wazīr and proclaims his titles. 2. A kind of ḥuḳḳa used by faḳīrs. 3. Flour made into dough for the preparation of sweetmeats.

s.m. See s.v. مي mai, 'wine.' s.f. Mother, &c. ( = mā, q.v.); — a woman's breast. s.m. (in Prosody) A molossus or metrical foot of three long syllables; — a magical or mystical formula; a name of Brahmā, of Vishṇu, and of Śiva.

more: n. m. S. मातृक The bride's paternal home. maikā basānā , Wom. v. n. To leave her father-in-law's and live with her parents. maike-vāliyāṅ , n. f. maike-vāle , n. m. The relations of the bride on the mother's side.
s. m. Kindred, relations, motherʼs family.

s.m., or maike-wāliyāṅ, s.f. A wife's maternal relations or kindred.

ety: prob. S. मलं; but cf. mailā
def: s.f. Dirt, filth; pollution; scum; rust; (met.) sadness; vexation, displeasure
more: n. m. 1. Inclination; tendency; bent; bias. 2. Partiality; fondness ( chāh , 1). mail mail , A call to an elephant to make him sit.

n. m. S. मल Dirt; scum; rust. Āṅkh meṅ mail, aur us meṅ mail nahīṅ . Prov. mail chhāṅṭnā, yā kāṭnā , v. a. 1. To wash (clothes). 2. To clarify; purify. mail-khorā , n. m. 1. An under-vestment. 2. An apron. 3. Cast-off clothes. mail kā bail banānā , v. a. To multiply words ( bāl kā kammal banānā ). mail lānā , To be sorry; to take offence.

s. f. Dirt, filth, rust, scum. ميل بيٿهنا mail baiṭhnā, To gather a crust. ميل چهانٿنا mail chhānṭnā, To fine, strain, purify, clarify. ميل خوره mail-ḵẖora, 1. An under vestment (as, under petticoat, &c.), an apron (dakh. Harris ). 2. A saddle-cloth. ميل کاٿنا mail kāṭnā, To refine, to purify.
subs: (-par), Dirt, &c. to settle (or); to gather a crust; to become rusty
(-kī), To fine, refine; to strain; to purify; to clarify; to wash (clothes)
s.m. A coloured garment worn over the clothes to keep the dirt off them; an apron; — an under-vestment (as a petticoat, &c.); — a garment which has become dirty and discoloured; — cast-off clothes
'To turn mail into bail'; to multiply words
or mail-kućailā, adj. contrac. of mailā-kućailā, q.v.
v.n. To be sad, or sorry; to be vexed, or displeased.

wety: Borrowed from Arabic مِيل (mīl), from Latin mīlle passuum.

ety: corr. fr. the English
def: s.m. A mile (i.q. mīl, q.v.). part. = milkar or milke, q.v.
subs: adv. = mile-jule, q.v. part. Having united, having combined, &c.; — adv. In a body, in combination, in unity; together; unitedly; narmonionsly, on good terms
v.n. To go, or journey together; — to live in harmony
To dwell together in unity or harmony.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic مِيل (mīl), from Latin mīlle passuum.

ety: inf. n. of مال (for ميل
def: s.m. Inclination (to), leaning; tendency; penchant; bent; bias; impulse; — wish, desire; — attachment, partiality, fondness, affection, love; — aptitude
subs: (-kī t̤araf), To feel inclination, or attachment, &c. (for), to be partial (to); to associate (with)
(-kī t̤araf, or -par), To incline or lean (to or towards); to have, or to show, an inclination, or a partiality (for), to love; — to close in (with, or upon).
wety: Borrowed from Arabic مِيل (mīl), from Latin mīlle passuum.

ety: act, part. of ميل 'to incline,' &c.
def: adj. Inclining (to or towards, -kī t̤araf, or -par), leaning; inclined; propense, having a propensity, or inclination, or partiality (for); biassed; fond (of), taking delight (in), addicted (to); apt
subs: v.t. To make (one) inclined (to, -kī t̤araf), to incline, bend, bias, &c.
(-kī t̤araf, or -par), To be inclined (to, or towards), to incline, lean; to be addicted (to), &c.

ety: Prk. मइलअं; S. मलिन+कं
def: adj. (f. -ī), Dirty, filthy; foul; soiled; defiled; nasty; — sad, troubled, vexed, annoyed; — inferior, low, mean; — s.m. Dirt, filth, &c. (i.q. mail, q.v.); — blood; — a man of loose character; a gambler; a roué; a blackleg; a sodomite, or catamite; a pilferer, a thief
more: adj. 1. Dirty; foul; defiled. 2. Inferior; low; mean. mailā , n. m. 1. Filth; dirt. 2. Blood. 3. A man of loose character. 4. A gambler. 5. A sodomite or catamite. 6. A fornicator. 7. A petty pilferer; thief. mailā-pan , n. m. Dirtiness; foulness. māilā k. , v. a. 1. To dirty; soil. 2. To go to stool. mailā h. , v. n. 1. To become dirty, rusty. Sonā ho to kas dharūṅ, motī dharūṅ piroë. Bā'ā sā jubnā kit dharūṅ, nit uṭh mailā hoë . Gold I might safe put by, and pearls bored. I might string, But budding breasts how keep, their bloom's e'er on the wing. 2. To spoil; fade; go to waste. maile sir se h. , v. n. To have the monthly course.

adj. Dirty, nasty, foul, defiled. ميلا کرنا mailā karnā, To dirty, tarnish, foul. ميلا هوجانا mailā hojānā, To tarnish, to become turbid, to be dirty. ميلا کچيلا mailā kuchailā ( कुचेल Dressed in dirty garments) adj. Dirty.
subs: or kućīlā, adj. (f. -ī), Dirty and ill-clad, filthy and ragged; dirty, foul, polluted (i.q. mailā)
v.t. To dirty, soil, befoul; — v.n. To pass excrement; to go to stool
To be sad or dejected, to be troubled, or vexed, or annoyed
v.n. To be or become dirty, or foul, or polluted; to become turbid; to become rusty; to tarnish; to spoil; to fade; to go to waste
v.n. 'To be with the head unwashed,' to be in a state of menstruation.

ety: mailā, q.v.+pan = Prk. प्पणं = S. त्वं (Vedic त्वनं)
def: s.m. Dirtiness, filthiness of condition; foulness, pollutedness, nastiness.

s.m. = P ميمنت maimanat, s.f. (for A. ميمنة, n. of place fr. يمن 'to take to the right,' &c.), The right side or hand; the right wing (of an army); — fortune; prosperity; happiness (opp. to maisara, q.v.; and cf. maimūn).
more: s. m. The right wing of an army.

def: s.f. = P ميمنه maimana, s.m. (for A. ميمنة, n. of place fr. يمن 'to take to the right,' &c.), The right side or hand; the right wing (of an army); — fortune; prosperity; happiness (opp. to maisara, q.v.; and cf. maimūn).
more: (from يمن Heb. and Aram. ) s. f. Fortune, prosperity, happiness.

def: s.f. (dialec.) = maibhā, q.v.; — 2˚ contrac. of maiṅ bhayā (i.q. maiṅbhā).

ety: pass. part. of يمن 'to be fortunate,' &c.
def: part. adj. Fortunate, auspicious; happy; prosperous; august; — s.m. A proper name; — an ape, a monkey, a baboon; — membrum virile.
more: (part. pass. of يمن) 1. adj. Fortunate, auspicious, prosperous. 2. s. m. A baboon, monkey. 3. Membrum virile.

ety: S. मामिका
def: s.f. Maternal uncle's wife, aunt ( = māmī); — a respectful term of address to elderly women (prob. for māʼī).
s.f. The fruit of the tamarisk tree.
s. f. An aunt. See مامي māmī.
s.f. corr. of maiyā, or māʼī, 'mother,' q.v. s.f. Mother (i.q. mā, or māʼī); — (voc.) O mother!

ety: Prk. मयणो; S. मदनः
def: s.m. A name of Kāmdev, the god of love; — the plant Vangueria spinosa; — a kind of short grass which grows on rich and inundated land, and chokes the crops
subs: s.m. The fruit of the plant Vangueria spinosa (used as an emetic); — nux vomica (syn. jauzuʼl. qai).

ety: Prk. महं, for महिं, gen. of अहं = S. अहम् 'I'
def: pron. I
subs: To be egotistical, to be full of oneself.

def: s.f. = maiyā, q.v. s.f. Mother (i.q. mā, or māʼī); — (voc.) O mother!
more: n. f. 1. A starling; Gracula religiosā . 1. Terī bārī umar, bāṅke nainā, Chāl chalat jaise murlā kī, bāteṅ kare jaise mainā . Song. You're in your youth's ripe bloom, your sidelong glances glow with heat, Your movements have the peacock's grace, your tones as maina's sweet. 2. Ek nār tarvar se utrī, sir par vāke pāoṅ, Aisī nār ku-nār ko maiṅ-nā dekhan jāūṅ . Riddle. Ans. Mainā. 2. A term of endearment addressed to children. Wom. mainā-pavī . The grey headed mainā; temenuchus malabaricus. mainā sārik . The common mainā; acridetheres tristis. āgā mainā , n. f. A superior talking mainā. ablaq mainā, ablakā ; E. suroīṅ . The pied Starling; sturnopastor contra. bāmanī mainā, puhaiā, pābiyā, pawī, brāhmanī mainā . The black-headed mainā; temenuchus pagodarum. pahāṛiā mainā . The Nepal hill-maina; Eulabes intermedia. teliā mainā. Sturnus vulgaris ; the common Starling. gulābī mainā . The rose-c…
s. f. Name of a bird, a kind of jay. (Coracias indica). ¶ mīnā, A cast of Hindūs, professedly thieves.

def: s.m. = main, q.v. s.f. Maternal uncle's wife, aunt ( = māmī); — a respectful term of address to elderly women (prob. for māʼī).

ety: prob. Prk. मयणा; S. मदना; cf. S. madana-sārikā
def: s.f. A kind of starling, Gracula religiosa (i.q. sārikā); — a term of endearment used by women in addressing children
subs: The pied starling, Sturnopastor contra (syn. ablakā)
(or brāhmanī) mainā, The black-headed Mainā, Temenuchus pagodarum
(or pahāṛī) mainā, The hill-Mainā of Nepal, Eulabes intermedia

ety: fr. menā
def: s.m. Name of a mountain (son of Hima-vat by Menā, and said to have alone retained his wings when Indra clipped those of the other mountains: according to some this mountain was situated between the southern point of the Indian peninsula and Lankā); — name of a Daitya or demon.
s.f. = maibhā, q.v. s.f. Stepmother.

ety: i.q. meḍh, q.v.
def: s.m. (dialec.) Gleanings of corn left on a field.
s.m. See s.v. main. s.f. Maternal uncle's wife, aunt ( = māmī); — a respectful term of address to elderly women (prob. for māʼī).
more: n. m. S. मदन फल A fruit used in medicine; Vanguieria spinosa , Wat. ( indrāin , 1).
s. m. Name of a fruit used in medicine. (Vangueria spinosa: Roxb. ).

ety: Prk. मणसिला or मणसिलो?; S. मनः+शिला or शिलः
def: s.m. Red orpiment, realgar, red arsenic, Arsenicum rubrum, the Sandaraca of Pliny (cf. mansal, and mansīl).

ety: cf. meṅṛ, and meṅṛā
def: s.m. (dialec.) A kind of platform or scaffold erected in a field (where a person is stationed to watch the crops); — a ramp; — a kind of harrow
subs: (with loc.) To pass the harrow over a field (in order to break the clods)
To take a turn, to turn over on the side (in sleeping).

ety: act. part. of ميس 'to walk with an elegant air,' &c.; and of مأس 'to breed dissensions,' &c.
def: adj. & s.m. Walking elegantly or proudly, waving from side to side; — one who so walks; — a whisperer, talebearer, calumniator.

ety: for A. ميسرة, n. of place fr. يسر 'to be easy, or fortunate,' &c.
def: s.m. The left side; left wing (of an army).
more: s. m. Left wing of an army.

ety: for A. معيشة, inf. n. of عاش (for عيش
def: s.f. Living, sustenance, victuals, daily food; the means of life or subsistence, livelihood; way of life.
more: n. f. 1. Livelihood. 2. Daily food; the necessaries of life.
(from عيش Heb. ) s. f. Subsistence, livelihood, living, way of life.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian معیشت (ma'īšat), from Arabic مَعِيشَة (maʕīša).

ety: corr. of ميشؤُم mashʼūm, pass. part. of شأم 'to be or become unlucky, or inauspicious,' &c.
def: part. adj. Unlucky, unfortunate, ill-omened, inauspicious; — perverse; execrable; disagreeable.
more: (for مش‍ﯝوم part. pass. of شؤم) adj. Unfortunate, unlucky, disagreeable.

ety: pass. part. of يسر 'to be made easy,' &c.
def: part. adj. Made easy, facilitated; easy; prosperous, going on successfully.
more: (part. pass. of يسر) adj. Facilitated, made easy, prosperous, going on successfully. Plur. مسورات maisūrāt, Prosperous affairs, good fortune.

def: s.m. pl. (of maisūr, q.v.), Prosperous affairs; good fortune.
s.m. (dialec.) = māʼekā, q.v. s.m. Mother's house, the maternal mansion (applied only to that of a woman).
adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Relating or belonging to Mithilā (the modern Tirhut); — an inhabitant, or a native, of Mithilā.
more: adj. An inhabitant of Mithilā or Tirhut.

def: s.f. An epithet of Sītā daughter of Janak, king of Mithilā); — the language of Mithilā or Tirhut (a dialect of Hindī).

ety: fr. mithuna
def: s.m. Pairing, coition, copulation, carnal intercourse, dalliance; — marriage, matrimony; — social intercourse; union, connection.
wety: Learned borrowing from Sanskrit मैथुन (maithuna).

def: adj. & s.m. (f. -inī), Having sexual intercourse; sexually united; — one who has had carnal intercourse with a woman.
adj. Proceeding from sexual passion; caused by the desire for sexual intercourse; relating to copulation.

ety: fr. mitra
def: adj. Coming, or derived, from a friend; given by a friend; of or belonging to a friend; friendly, amicable, well-disposed, kind, benevolent, affectionate; — s.m. 'A friend to all creatures,' a Brāhmaṅ who has arrived at the highest stage of human perfection; — a particular mixed caste or degraded tribe (the offspring of an outcast Vaiśya); a man of that caste; — epithet of the twelfth astrological yoga; — the anus, fundament; — (for S. maitram), friendship; — name of the seventeenth Nakshatra; — evacuation of excrement (presided over by the god Mitra).

ety: fr. maitrī
def: adj. Of or relating to a friend; friendly; — s.m. = maitreyak, q.v. s.m. Name of a particular mixed caste or degraded tribe (the offspring of a Vaideha father by an Ayogavī woman); an individual of that caste (whose business is to praise great men, and to announce the dawn by ringing a bell); (hence) a crier or chanter of the hours.

s.m. Name of a particular mixed caste or degraded tribe (the offspring of a Vaideha father by an Ayogavī woman); an individual of that caste (whose business is to praise great men, and to announce the dawn by ringing a bell); (hence) a crier or chanter of the hours.
s.f. Friendship, friendliness, good-will; — association, intimate connection, union; relationship.

ety: Prk. माइआ; S. मातृका
def: s.f. Mother (i.q. mā, or māʼī); — (voc.) O mother!

Mah. n. H. māṅjhā a bed. The affianced bride and bridegroom remain closeted in their respective houses some days before marriage, during which period they are not seen by strangers or by their relations.

ety: for A. معية, abst. s. fr. مع maʻ, q.v.
def: s.f. The being together; conjunction; association, society; co-existence.

r: maiyit, mait, maiwit, mawīt, faʻīl, faiʻil
ety: for maiwit, for mawīt, verb. adj., of the form فعيل faʻīl, or, by transposition, فَيْعِل faiʻil, fr. مات (for موت
def: adj. Dead; — s.f. A dead body, a corpse.
more: (from موت Heb. and Aram. ) s. f. A dead body.

ety: Prk. मज्जं; S. मद्यं; or fr. S. मृदुः
def: adj. Soft, mellow, ripe.

ety: see māṅj, māṅjhā
def: s.f. Foam of the water of the early rains.
s.m. corr. of مزه maza, q.v.
f. ( मातृजात ) adj. Born of the same mother.

ety: n. of place fr. جال (for جول
def: s.f. Place, room, scope; opportanity; — strength, power, ability, authority
more: n. f. جول going round. Authority; power; ability. majāl rakhnā, yā h. , v. n. To have authority or power. kyā majāl hai! intj. What can he do?
(noun of place from جول Heb. and Chald. ) s. f. Power, ability, strength.
subs: (-kī), or majāl honā (-ko of person, and kī of thing), To have the power (to), &c.
What power has he? What can he do?
wety: From Arabic مَجَال (majāl)

def: s.f. pl. (of majlis), Sessions; assemblies; conferences; convivial conversations.
more: s. plur. (of مجلس) Conferences, assemblies, convivial conversation.

def: s.m. pl. (of majmaʻ, q.v.), Assemblies, congregations, &c.
more: s. plur. (of مجمع from جمع Chald. ) Congregations.

ety: A. mā, 'what' + جري jarā, 'happened'
def: s.m. An accident, event, incident, occurrence, adventure; thing past
more: n. m. ما what and جري happened. An event ( sarguzasht , 1).
(comp. of ما What, and past tense of جري Happened) s. m. State, condition, circumstance, an event, incident, occurrence, adventure, accident.
subs: condition, circumstance. (N.B. This word inflects in Hindūstānī; e.g. obl. mājre.)

def: adj. & s.m. (f. -jāʼī), See s.v. mā, 'mother.' s.m. (in Prosody) A molossus or metrical foot of three long syllables; — a magical or mystical formula; a name of Brahmā, of Vishṇu, and of Śiva.
more: m. ( मातृजात ) adj. Born of the same mother.

ety: n. of place fr. جاز (for جوز
def: s.m. Trope, figure, metaphor, simile, allegory.
more: adj. جوز passed as valid. Competent; legally competent; authorized; warranted. majāz , n. m. Authority; competency. majāz h. , v. n. To have authority, etc.; to be legally authorized. gair-majāz , adj. Unwarranted; not authorized.
(noun of place from جوز Heb. ) 1. adj. Lawful, admissible. 2. Superficial, insincere, feigned. 3. s. Metaphor. مجازًا majāzaṉ, adj. Metaphorically, allegorically.

ety: for A. mujāz, pass. part. of اجاز 'to make, or hold to be, allowable,' &c., iv of جاز (for جوز
def: adj. & s.m. Allowable, admissible, lawful, authorized, warranted, licensed; legally competent (syn. jāʼiz); — artificial, feigned, insincere, false; superficial; political; worldly; profane (as opp. to divine love); — illusory, delusive; — permission, authority, licence; competency
subs: v.n. To be allowed, to be legally authorized, &c.

ety: acc. of majāz
def: adv. Figuratively, metaphorically; — (and in H. fr. majāz, corr. of mujāz), legally, lawfully; — superficially; insincerely, hypocritically.

ety: rel. n. fr. majāz
def: adj. Tropical, figurative, metaphorical; allusive; hyperbolical; unreal; — feigned, insincere, &c. ( = majāz, q.v.); — (and, in H., fr. majāz, corr. of mujāz), authorized, licensed, competent, &c. ( = majāz, q.v.); — adv. Superficially, &c. ( = majāzaṉ, q.v.)
more: adv. 1. Artificial; false; spurious. 2. Illusive; delusive. 3. Mundane; worldly. Ishq-i-majāzī se ishq-i-haqīqī hāsil hotā hai . Prov. From love profane grows love divine. 4. Figurative; metaphoric; allegorical.
adv. Superficially. adj. Metaphorick, figurative, allusive, hyperbolical, not real; mundane.
subs: An authorized vendor, a licensed dealer.

ety: pass. part. of جبر 'to compel, constrain,' &c.
def: part. Compelled, constrained, forced, necessitated; helpless; oppressed
more: adj. جبر constrained. Constrained ( lā-chār , 1, 2, 4). majbūr k. , v. a. To constrain; coerce; compel. majbūr h. , v. a. To be compelled ( lā-chār h .). majbūr'an, majbūrī se , adv. Compulsorily; of necessity. majbūran ikhtiyār k. , v. n. To adopt compulsorily.
(part. act. of جبر Heb. and Chald. ) adj. Constrained, forced, compelled, necessitated, helpless, oppressed.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مجبور (majbūr), from Arabic مَجْبُور (majbūr).

ety: acc. of majbūr
def: adv. Compulsorily; of necessity; having no alternative (syn. majbūrī-se).

ety: fr. majbūr, q.v.
def: s.f. Compulsion, constraint; powerlessness, helplessness (syn. lā-ćārī)
more: n. f. 1. Want of power ( lāchārī , 1, 2). 2. Constraint; compulsion; coercion.
subs: adv. In consequence of compulsion or constraint; from helplessness; of necessity.

def: adj. = H مجبوتي मजबूती majbūtī, s.f. corr. of مضبوط maẓbūt̤, & مضبوطي maẓbūt̤ī, qq.v.
s.f. = H مجبوت मजबूत majbūt, adj. corr. of مضبوط maẓbūt̤, & مضبوطي maẓbūt̤ī, qq.v.

ety: inf. n. of مجد 'to excel in glory,' &c.
def: s.m. Glory, grandeur, greatness; splendour; the honour derived from ancestors
more: (Heb. and Aram. ) s. m. Glory, grandeur, splendour, the honour derived from ancestors. ذوالمجد ẕu-l-majd, adj. Glorious.

ety: prob. fr. S. मध्यं; Prk. मञ्झं
def: s.m. (dialec.) A mixed soil of clay and sand.
more: n. m. A mixed soil of clay and sand. Carnegy .

def: s.m. corr. of مزدور mazdūr, q.v.

ety: Prk. मज्झं; S. मध्यं
def: adj. & s.m. Middle

ety: Prk. मज्झं; S. मध्यं
def: s.m. (Old H.) = madhya, q.v. adj. Middlemost, middle, central, mid.

ety: Prk. मज्झे, मज्झि, मज्झ, Ap. Prk. मज्झहिं; S. मध्ये
def: adv. In the middle of the stream, in mid-stream.

ety: app. the conj. part. of an unused verb majhānā
def: adv. Through the middle or centre; across, athwart.
s.m. = مجہله majhala.

ety: for A. مجہلة majhalat, v.n. fr. جہل 'to be ignorant,' &c.
def: s.m. Anything indicative of ignorance or folly; anything which incites to foolish or wrong conduct; wrangling; a dilemma, difficulty (cf jhamelā).

ety: Prk. मज्झलओ, or मज्झिल्लओ; S. मध्य + ल, or इल + कः
def: adj. (f. -ī), Middle, &c. ( = majholā, q.v.)
subs: adv. In the middle, in the midst.

def: s.m. (old H.) = majh, q.v. (cf. māṅj, māṅjh). adj. & s.m. Middle

ety: n. of place fr. جحر 'to enter its burrow or hole' (a lizard, &c.
def: s.m. A hiding-place, place of refuge, an asylum.
more: (noun of place, from جحر) s. m. An asylum, a hiding-place.

ety: Prk. मज्झालो, or मज्झल्लो; S. मध्य + आलः, or अ
def: s.m. The middle, centre; — adv. In the middle, in the midst (of, -ke), in.
more: s. m. The middle, centre; in the middle, in.

ety: Prk. मज्झालओ, or मज्झल्लोओ; S. मध्य + आल, or ल + कः
def: adj. (f. -ī), Middle; middlemost; intervening, &c. (syn. majhlā, q.v.)
more: adj. H. maṅjh middle. Middle; intervening.
subs: adv. In the middle, in the midst.

def: s. f. The midstream.

ety: Prk. मज्झे, मज्झि, मज्झ, Ap. Prk. मज्झहिं; S. मध्ये
def: adv. In the middle of the stream, in mid-stream.

ety: Prk. मज्झइल्लआ; S. मध्य+इल+कः
def: adj. (f. -ī) = majholā, q.v.; — majhelī, s.f. Noon; midnight

ety: Prk. मज्झइल्लिआ; S. मध्य + इल + इका
def: s.f. = majholī, q.v. s.f. A kind of cart or carriage of middling size, on two wheels (drawn by bullocks).
more: (see مجهولي majholī ) s. f. A small cart.

ety: Prk. मज्झे, मज्झि, मज्झ, Ap. Prk. मज्झहिं; S. मध्ये
def: adv. In the middle of the stream, in mid-stream.

ety: dialec.
def: adj. (f. -ī) = majhelā, q.v. adj. (f. -ī) = majholā, q.v.; — majhelī, s.f. Noon; midnight
adj. ( Butchers ) Eight rupees. mājhī āsir , ( Butchers ) Eighteen rupees. mājhī mainā , ( Butchers ) Twentyeight rupees.

ety: Prk. मज्झउल्लओ; S. मध्य+उल + कः
def: adj. (f. -ī), Middle, middlemost; second of three; intermediate; middling.

ety: Prk. मज्झउल्लिआ; S. मध्य + उल + इका
def: s.f. A kind of cart or carriage of middling size, on two wheels (drawn by bullocks).
more: (perhaps, from मध्यम see منجهولا manjholā ) s. f. See مجهيلي majhelī.

ety: pass. part. of جہد 'to harass, distress,' &c.
def: part. adj. Oppressed with labour or vexation; severely affected; distressed, afflicted, troubled, harassed, embarrassed, inconvenienced; fatigued, wearied; — s.m. Care, trouble; endeavour; diligence; study; work, labour, toil.
(part. pass. of جـﮩد) adj. Oppressed with labour or vexation. (subst.) Care, trouble, endeavour, diligence.

ety: pass. part. of جہل 'to be ignorant,' &c.
def: adj. Unknown; of unknown origin (a man); (in Gram.) passive (voice, or verb); an epithet of the letters و and ي, when the former has the sound of o in 'bone,' and the latter that of e in 'fête'; — useless, indolent, lazy, idle; — s.m. (in Gram.), The passive voice; — a lazy fellow, a drone
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مجهول (majhūl), from Arabic مَجْهُول (majhūl).

def: adj. جـﮩل was ignorant. 1. Unknown. 2. Indolent; lazy. majhūl-ul-nasab . Of unknown parentage or descent. raqam majhūl , ( Math .) An unknown quantity; x. fel majhūl . The passive voice.
more: (part. pass. of جـﮩل) 1. Unknown, little known. 2. (subst.) Passive voice. 3. Applied to the letters و and ي when the former has the sound of o in bone, and the latter that of a in name. 4. (In Hind.) Indolent, lazy, idle. مجـﮩول النسب majʼhūlu-n-nasab, adj. Unknown of lineage. مجـﮩول مطلق majʼhūl-i-mut̤laḳ, Very indolent or useless.

ety: majhūl, q.v.+ī = Zend i = S. इ
def: s.f. Ignorance; — indolence, laziness, idleness, apathy.
s. f. Ignorance, indolence, laziness, idleness, apathy.

ety: act. part. of مجد 'to excel in glory,' &c.
def: adj. Conspicuous for honour, or nobility, or glory; glorious, honourable; eminently beneficent and humane.

ety: v.n. fr. مجد 'to excel in glory'
def: adj. Glorious, honourable, noble; exalted, high
more: adj. Noble; great. kalām majīd , The Kuran.
(from مجد Heb. and Aram. ) adj. Glorious, honourable, noble.
subs: The glorious word (of God), the Qorʼān.

def: (for A. ماجدة), adj. fem. of mājid, q.v.

s.f. corr. of manzil, q.v. s.f. A place for alighting, a place for the accommodation of travellers, a caravansary, an inn, a hotel; a house, lodging, dwelling, mansion, habitation, station; a mansion of the moon; story or floor (of a house); deck (of a ship); — a day's journey; — a stage (in travelling, or in the divine life); — place of destination, goal; boundary, end, limit
subs: adv. From stage to stage; by stages; — at every atage
v.t. To convey (one) to his destination; to convey (one) to his last resting-place, to perform the obsequies of; — to bring (a thing) home to (a person), to prove to
(-ko), To lay down (a corpse) for a while on the way to its resting-place
or manzil karnā, To finish a journey; to get over a stage; to go one stage
v.n. To finish a difficult task
s.f. The goal of desire; goal, destination
To reach (one's) destination; — to gain (one's) object
A stiff journey or stage; a long stage or march.

ety: S. मञ्जीर + कं
def: s.m. A small cymbal; a tinkling ornament for the feet or toes (also written manjīrā).

ety: corr. fr. the English
def: s.m. A magistrate
n. m. Cor. of Eng. Magistrate. The committing officer. majisṭreṭ ālā . A consul; chief magistrate. anāṛī majistret , An honorary magistrate.
s.f. The office of magistrate; magistracy.
n. f. The office.

ety: Prk. मज्जितो; S. मार्जितः, or मृजितः, rt. मृज्
def: adj. Rubbed, worn; soiled, tarnished, fouled, dimmed; no longer new; second-hand; sold at an under price, cheap; bought at second-hand.
more: adj. 1. Cheap, that which is sold at an under price. 2. Bought at second hand.

def: s.f. The drug Rubia manjith, the root of which yields a red dye; — madder.
n. f. S. मिञ्जिष्टा A drug which yields a red dye; Rubia cordifolia , Wat.
s. f. Name of a drug used for dying red. (Rubia manjith: Roxb. ).
s.f. Marrow; pith; sap.
s.m. Dipping, diving, immersion bathing, ablution; sinking, drowning.
v.t. (old H.) To immerse, submerge; to bathe.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مَجْنُون (majnūn), from Arabic مَجْنُون (majnūn, “crazy”, literally “possessed by a jinn”).

ety: n. of place fr. جلس 'to sit'
def: s.f. An assembly, congregation, company, party, meeting; convivial meeting; convention, congress, council, conference; — an assembly of ṣūfīs and dancing dervishes
more: n. f. جلس sat. 1. A party; council (جماعت 1, 2). 2. An assembly of sufīs and dancing dervises. majlis barāë rifāh-i-ām . A meeting in the public interest. majlis-khānah . 1. A town hall. 2. A ball room. majlis-i-raqs o sarod . A ball; concert. majlis-i-ilmī . A literary association. majlis k. , v. a. 1. To convene a meeting or assembly. 2. To commemorate the martyrdom of Imām Husain . ( Shīās ). majlis meṅ sharīk h. , v. n. To join an assembly; attend a meeting. mīr-majlis , n. m. The president or head of a society.
(noun of place, from جلس) s. f. An assembly, a company, congregation, party, convention.
subs: To convene a meeting or assembly; — (among Shīʻas) to commemorate the martyrdom of Imām Husain
To join an assembly; to attend a meeting
s.m. The president or head of a society.

ety: rel. n. fr. majlis
def: s.m. A person invited to an assembly; an assessor.
more: n. m. One invited to an assembly.

ety: n. of place from جمع 'to collect,' &c.
def: s.m. A place in which people collect or assemble; place of meeting or rendezvous; an assembly, a congregation, concourse; convention; meeting, rendezvous; a collection, heap, pile; — a book of miscellanies, a magazine
more: n. m. جمع collecting. 1. A place of meeting or rendezvous. 2. An assembly ( bhīṛ ). majma-i-khilāf qānūn, yā nā-jāiz , G. G. An unlawful assemblage. majma-i-ām . A public assemblage or meeting. majma-i-ām meṅ mukhil h. , v. n. G. G. To disturb a public assembly. majma-i-mazhabī . A religious assembly. majma-i-mufsidān . A riotous assembly. kisī bad-nīatī se majma ikhaṭṭā k . To assemble with evil intent.
(noun of place, from جمع Chald. from Heb. analog. to ) s. m. Place of assembling, assembly, collection, rendezvous, assemblage.
subs: To collect a body of people together
s.m. The place where the two seas meet; the meeting of two seas.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مَجْمَع (majma'), borrowed from Arabic مَجْمَع (majmaʕ), from جَمَعَ (jamaʕa). First attested in c. 1657 as Middle Hindi مجمع (mjm').

ety: for A. مجمعة majmaʻat, fr. majmaʻ
def: s.m. A place in which people collect or assemble; place of meeting or rendezvous; an assembly, a congregation, concourse; convention; meeting, rendezvous; a collection, heap, pile; — a book of miscellanies, a magazine
more: (noun of place, from جمع Chald. from Heb. analog. to ) s. m. Place of assembling, assembly, collection, rendezvous, assemblage.
subs: To collect a body of people together
s.m. The place where the two seas meet; the meeting of two seas.

ety: pass. part. of جمع 'to collect,' &c.
def: part. adj. Collected, brought together, assembled, convened; included, contained; collected, composed, content, tranquil; — collective, aggregate; all, the whole, total, every one
subs: adj. Collected in mind, at ease in mind.

ety: for A. مجموعة majmūʻat, fem. of majmūʻ
def: adj. Collected, &c. ( = majmūʻ, q.v.); — s.m. The collective mass (of), the whole (of), the aggregate (of); the sum (of); a crowd, an assembly; a collection; meeting; a compendium; a body (of laws); a magazine; a miscellany; — a form (in printing); — a compound perfume
more: collected. 1. An aggregate (جمع 1). 2. A collection; compendium. 3. A body of laws; a code. majmūah , p. adj. Brought together; collected. majmūah silsilah ; S. एढीफल ( Alg .) The sum of the terms. majmūah-qavānīn . A code; constitution. majmūë kā itr . An attar extracted from different scents. majmū'ī , adj. Collective; aggregate. majmūī qīmat . Aggregate value. majmūī nambar . An aggregate number.

(part. pass. of جمع) 1. s. m. A crowd, assembly. 2. A compound perfume. 3. A collection, meeting, magazine, compendium, aggregate. 4. A form (in printing). 5. adj. Collected, assembled, contained, all, the whole. مجموعه دار majmūʼaﻌ-dār (record-keeper) A title given to the servants of a ḳānūn-go.
subs: s.m. 'A record-keeper'; a title given to the servants of a qānūn-go
s.m. The sum of a series
s.m. A body of laws, a code
A perfume extracted from different scents.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مَجْمُوعَه (majmū'a), borrowed from Arabic مَجْمُوعَة (majmūʕa), from مَجْمُوع (majmūʕ), from جَمَعَ (jamaʕa).

ety: fr. majmūʻ
def: adj. Collective, aggregate ( = majmūʻa)

ety: prob. S. मान्य + nā = lā = Prk. लओ = S. ल+कः; cf. mānjnā
def: s.m. Honour, esteem (used chiefly by women).
more: Hin. Wom. n. m. Cor. of A. mavāznah weight. Honor; esteem. Hamārā do kauṛī kā mājnā kar dīyā .

def: n. m. lit . possessed by a demon. 1. One whom love has turned mad. 2. A very thin weak person.

ety: pass. part. of جنّ 'to be possessed by a jinn, or an evil spirit'
def: part. Possessed by an evil spirit; insane, demented, mad; madly or desperately in love; — s.m. A demoniac; maniac; a fanatic; one whom love has turned mad; name of a celebrated Eastern lover (whose amours with Lailī are the subject of a famous poem by Niz̤āmī); — a very thin and weak person.
more: (part. pass. of جنّ Heb. and Chald. ) 1. adj. Insane, in love. 2. n. prop. The name of a celebrated lover, whose amours with Lailạ̄ are the subject of a poem by Niz̤āmī.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian مَجْنُون (majnūn), from Arabic مَجْنُون (majnūn, “crazy”, literally “possessed by a jinn”).

ety: for A. مجنونية, abst. s. fr. majnūn
def: s.f. Insanity; demoniac possession.

ety: pass. part. of جرح 'to wound,' &c.
def: part. adj. Wounded (syn. ghāʼil); — smitten, love-smitten
more: adj. جرح wounding. 1. Wounded. 2. Smitten by love. majrūh k . To wound; inflict a wound.
subs: v.t. To inflict a wound on, to wound
or ho-jānā, v.n. To be or become wounded.

ety: pass. part. of جصّ 'to plaster'
def: part. Plastered (with gypsum or lime).

adj. corr. of مضبوط maẓbūt̤, q.v. part. adj. & adv. Held firmly; possessed, occupied; — restrained, controlled; well regulated; moderated; disciplined; — firm, fixed, fast, tight; immovable; stable, steady; strong, stout; — resolute; cogent, valid; — firmly, tightly, &c.
subs: v.t. To make firm, or strong, &c.; to make fast, to fix.

ety: v.n. fr. جوف 'to be hollow,' &c.; or fr. jauf, 'belly'
def: adj. Wide in the jauf or belly, large-bellied; thick.
more: adj. 1. (part. pass. II. of جوف Heb. and Chald. ) Hollow, concave, vaulted. 2. ¶ majūf, Thick, large-bellied.

def: s.f. (dialec.) corr. of maʻjūn, q.v. (cf. mājūm). part. Kneaded (as a mass of dough); — s.f. An electuary, a confection; a drug, or drugs, mixed up with honey, or inspissated juice or syrup, and generally containing opium, or hemp, or some other intoxicating ingredient.

ety: corr. of معجون maʻjūn, q.v.
def: s.f. (dialec.) A confection, &c.; — an inebriating preparation of bhaṅg with sugar and spices, formed into cakes.

ety: pass. part. of عجن 'to knead'
def: part. Kneaded (as a mass of dough); — s.f. An electuary, a confection; a drug, or drugs, mixed up with honey, or inspissated juice or syrup, and generally containing opium, or hemp, or some other intoxicating ingredient.
more: n. f. An intoxicating drug made of the extract of hemp and sugar; an electuary.
(part. pass. of عجن) s. f. An electuary medicine, confection.

def: A galnut.
= H مجورا मजूरा majūrā s.m. corr. of مزدور mazdūr, q.v.
= H مجور मजूर majūr, s.m. corr. of مزدور mazdūr, q.v.
more: s. m. Corrupt. of مزدور q. v.

def: s.f. corr. of mazdūrī, q.v.
